summary: I verified the Add Dark Mode Support feature against all 13 requirements using a **real headless Chrome browser** (I bundle-installed the missing gems and bypassed a stale chromedriver in PATH so Selenium Manager could fetch a driver matching Chrome 150). The committed system test passed (7 assertions covering toggle light↔dark, immediate application without reload, `aria-pressed` reflecting state, and cookie persistence across visits — R4–R8). I then ran a temporary CDP-driven test to exercise the subtle system-reactivity requirements: R1 (defaults to system=dark with no cookie), R10 (follows an OS scheme change when the user hasn't chosen — the `matchMedia` change listener fires and toggles the `.dark` class once the async event settles; my first assertion failed only because it checked before the event dispatched, which a probe confirmed), and R11 (a manual choice survives a later OS change). Static analysis covered the remainder: the toggle renders in both app layouts (sidebar + auth) so it's reachable everywhere (R3), a scan found **zero hardcoded light-only colors** since the app repaints via semantic tokens redeclared under `.dark` (R12), and `status_badge` always shows the status word with dark-mode ring/text variants (R13). No defects found — **verdict: pass**, and I cleaned up all temporary test files.
